jokes.text: Bow down before recyclops.
jokes.setup: Dwight introduces his recycling character with dramatic flair
jokes.punchline: The over-the-top superhero persona for basic recycling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight character moment - taking something mundane and making it epic. Strong character integration and memorable delivery.

jokes.text: Did you know that an old milk carton can be sawed in half and used as a planter?
jokes.setup: Dwight in superhero costume delivering helpful tips
jokes.punchline: The earnest environmental advice in dramatic costume
jokes.explanation: Solid character-driven humor showing Dwight's earnest nature even in costume.

jokes.text: Who has put a number seven plastic in a number four bin?
jokes.setup: Recyclops becoming more intense over time
jokes.punchline: Obsessive recycling enforcement with superhero anger
jokes.explanation: Shows the escalation of Recyclops character and Dwight's attention to detail. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: Recyclops will drown you in your overwatered lawns.
jokes.setup: Recyclops getting darker and more threatening
jokes.punchline: Environmental threat mixed with specific lawn care critique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from helpful to threatening. The specific 'overwatered lawns' detail makes it uniquely funny and quotable.

jokes.text: Recyclops will have his revenge.
jokes.setup: Narrator explains fictional planet attack storyline
jokes.punchline: Dwight taking the backstory seriously with dramatic revenge vow
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dwight's commitment to his fictional world-building.

jokes.text: I thought you were killed by polluticorn or something. Polluticorn wishes.
jokes.setup: Jim casually referencing Dwight's elaborate mythology
jokes.punchline: Dwight's immediate defensive response about fictional enemy
jokes.explanation: 'Polluticorn' is a great made-up word and Dwight's instant defensive response shows his investment in the character.

jokes.text: The thing I like most about recyclops is that he's creating a different world for our child. A world where you truly can be anything you want.
jokes.setup: Pam speaking earnestly about Dwight's ridiculous character
jokes.punchline: Taking Recyclops seriously as inspirational figure
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan from Pam treating the absurd situation with mock sincerity.

jokes.text: God bless you, recyclops and your cold robot heart.
jokes.setup: Pam continuing to speak earnestly about Recyclops
jokes.punchline: The contradictory 'blessing' of a 'cold robot heart'
jokes.explanation: Great contradiction in blessing someone's 'cold robot heart' - economical and memorable.

jokes.text: And it looks like you're taking a dump.
jokes.setup: Michael planning his wave for the shareholders meeting
jokes.punchline: Pam's blunt assessment of his posture
jokes.explanation: Simple visual humor with Pam's characteristic bluntness.

jokes.text: And then michael scott turns and waves to the crowd and the crowd goes wild.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his fantasy scenario
jokes.punchline: His grandiose expectations for a simple introduction
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael delusion of grandeur, perfectly in character.

jokes.text: please welcome michael 'the machine' scott.
jokes.setup: Michael fantasizing about his introduction
jokes.punchline: The self-appointed nickname 'the machine'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Michael giving himself a wrestling-style nickname.

jokes.text: Don't do the twirl. Lose the twirl. Twirl sucks. Michael, I hated the twirl.
jokes.setup: Multiple people reacting to Michael's performance
jokes.punchline: Universal hatred for something we didn't see
jokes.explanation: Good running bit with everyone piling on about the unseen twirl.

jokes.text: It's not even a twirl, it's a spin. I might do the spin.
jokes.setup: Everyone telling Michael not to do the twirl
jokes.punchline: Michael's semantic defense and continued determination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - finding a loophole in the criticism through semantics.

jokes.text: Okay, oscar, I don't need the whole enchilada just bullet points. Those are the bullet points.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Oscar to simplify his explanation
jokes.punchline: Oscar's deadpan response that it's already simplified
jokes.explanation: Great setup/punchline with Oscar's dry delivery deflating Michael's request.

jokes.text: Town cars are actually better though, better torque, better handling. He said limousine so... Check it out, guys. There's a limo down here.
jokes.setup: Dwight defending town cars as superior
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning his argument when he sees the limo
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dwight's instant flip when proven wrong.

jokes.text: A town car is something that a company sends when they're in trouble. A limousine is something that a company sends when they have cause for celebration.
jokes.setup: Michael creating his own business wisdom
jokes.punchline: The made-up distinction about corporate transportation
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael creating false business wisdom that sounds plausible.

jokes.text: It's like what high school kids take to prom on tv shows. This is so typical of management to spend money on this. Uh, they're a bunch of boobs.
jokes.setup: Oscar criticizing management spending on the limo
jokes.punchline: Not realizing he's criticizing the trip he's about to enjoy
jokes.explanation: Mild irony with Oscar's complaint about something he'll soon benefit from.

jokes.text: Hate to break it to you, oscar, but some of us like boobs.
jokes.setup: Oscar calling management 'boobs' meaning idiots
jokes.punchline: Kevin taking it literally as breasts
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin misunderstanding with juvenile but economical humor.

jokes.text: Calves, calves all the way.
jokes.setup: Kevin talking about liking body parts
jokes.punchline: His specific and unexpected preference for calves
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin character moment with his specific and weird preference.

jokes.text: and you're invited, and you're invited, and you, and you, and you, and you, and you, and you... Car seats eight. And you...
jokes.setup: Michael enthusiastically inviting everyone
jokes.punchline: Reality check interrupting his generous impulses
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Michael's enthusiasm being cut short by logistics.

jokes.text: The dunder mifflin stock symbol is dmi. Do you know what that stands for? Dummies, morons and idiots because that's what you'd have to be to own it.
jokes.setup: Oscar asking about the stock symbol meaning
jokes.punchline: His cynical backronym for the company stock
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good setup and a memorable, quotable punchline.

jokes.text: I wish the windows weren't tinted so people could see us in here.
jokes.setup: Michael enjoying the limo experience
jokes.punchline: Wanting to be seen rather than enjoying privacy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's vanity and need for attention.

jokes.text: Hey guys, do you think anyone might have had sex in here? Definitely. Sounds like it. Definitely. Look, they got pillows.
jokes.setup: Ryan asking an inappropriate question
jokes.punchline: Everyone immediately agreeing and providing evidence
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor with Ryan being inappropriate and others playing along.

jokes.text: That, that's bigger than my bed.
jokes.setup: Looking at the limo seating
jokes.punchline: Ryan revealing his sad living situation
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Ryan's poor living situation, economically delivered.

jokes.text: It doesn't seem like there's much of a point if the company's going under. But if the company doesn't go under, then we'll finally have all our contacts in one program.
jokes.setup: Ryan's logical objection to doing work
jokes.punchline: Jim's circular reasoning for why to do it anyway
jokes.explanation: Good workplace absurdity with Jim's non-logic for continuing pointless tasks.

jokes.text: Say your name is zamboni and I will say, 'well, we're sort of on thin ice.'
jokes.setup: Michael planning his prepared joke
jokes.punchline: The terrible zamboni/thin ice pun
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael bad pun, perfectly in character but objectively terrible.

jokes.text: Well, thank you and please call me... Continue to call me mr. Scott.
jokes.setup: Michael about to be humble
jokes.punchline: Switching back to formal title mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael character moment with the false humility quickly abandoned.

jokes.text: Oh, it was awesome. Very, very sublime.
jokes.setup: Being asked about the limo ride
jokes.punchline: Michael's pretentious word choice
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael trying to sound sophisticated with unusual word choices.

jokes.text: Ah, your eminence.
jokes.setup: Being introduced to a former congressman
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate religious title
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Michael using wrong formal address.

jokes.text: I wanted to swing by the garment district and pick up a few crates of my shirts. I got a shirt guy.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining why he wanted to go to New York
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of buying shirts by the crate
jokes.explanation: Good absurd detail about having a 'shirt guy' and buying by the crate.

jokes.text: Oh, what a great idea and lose my job. No, thank you.
jokes.setup: Being encouraged to confront the board
jokes.punchline: Oscar's sarcastic response about job security
jokes.explanation: Good Oscar sarcasm showing his practical mindset.

jokes.text: How is he gonna have grandkids?
jokes.setup: Talk about telling grandkids about standing up
jokes.punchline: Kevin's blunt question about Oscar's prospects
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin inappropriate comment, perfectly economical and in character.

jokes.text: Nelly Furtado.
jokes.setup: Kevin asking about protecting famous people
jokes.punchline: The deflating reveal of protecting a minor celebrity
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax with the mundane celebrity reveal after the buildup.

jokes.text: This is not as much fun as I thought it would be. It was fun when we weren't on the brink of bankruptcy.
jokes.setup: Michael experiencing the hostile shareholder meeting
jokes.punchline: His naive surprise at the reality of corporate crisis
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's disconnection from reality.

jokes.text: Oh, if onlys and justs were candies and nuts then everyday would be erntedankfest.
jokes.setup: Someone making excuses about bathroom break
jokes.punchline: Dwight's mangled version of common saying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight malapropism turning 'Christmas' into 'erntedankfest' - highly quotable and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Yes!
jokes.setup: Being introduced to the shareholders
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate celebration
jokes.explanation: Perfect single-word reaction showing Michael's tone-deafness to the situation.

jokes.text: Okay, shouldn't be telling me that stuff and also shouldn't be taking two hour lunches without telling me.
jokes.setup: Phyllis oversharing about getting drunk at lunch
jokes.punchline: Jim trying to assert authority he doesn't have
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Jim's management struggles and his discomfort with personal information.

jokes.text: What's so funny? I'm a co-manager. That doesn't make you a boss.
jokes.setup: Jim asserting his authority
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's blunt dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good punchline with Phyllis cutting through Jim's title confusion.

jokes.text: Well, who... How did you... Who told you this? Ryan. Sorry, I'm plastered.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to find the source of rumors
jokes.punchline: Phyllis casually admitting she's drunk and revealing the source
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Phyllis being too honest about her drinking.

jokes.text: Who here believes that I have as much power as michael?
jokes.setup: Jim asking for validation of his authority
jokes.punchline: The implied silence/lack of response
jokes.explanation: Good awkward silence moment showing Jim's lack of real authority.

jokes.text: I forgot I have to support him no matter what. Close one.
jokes.setup: Jim's authority crisis
jokes.punchline: Pam's dry admission about spousal loyalty
jokes.explanation: Good Pam deadpan about marriage dynamics and supporting Jim.

jokes.text: In high school, I organized a walk out over standardized testing. Got over 500 students to just skip the sats. At the last second I chickened out, took it anyway, got a 1220.
jokes.setup: Oscar as a rebel organizer
jokes.punchline: The anticlimactic ending and specific score
jokes.explanation: Excellent character revelation showing Oscar's hypocrisy and the specific 1220 score makes it memorable.

jokes.text: I feel lachrymose.
jokes.setup: Oscar feeling emotional about speaking up
jokes.punchline: His pretentious word choice for sad
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar character moment with unnecessarily fancy vocabulary.

jokes.text: You're criminals! Hey, hey! I'm sorry these are not criminals. They're nice. They invited me to come here today, they invited me to their hospitality suite, where I had free food, and it was delicious.
jokes.setup: Angry accusation at the board
jokes.punchline: Michael's naive defense based on free food
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael missing the point and being swayed by minor perks.

jokes.text: Get this, you know how nice they are? They sent a stretch limo all the way to pick me up in scranton. Limousine?
jokes.setup: Michael defending the board's generosity
jokes.punchline: Shareholder's outrage at the wasteful spending
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Michael's evidence of kindness actually proving the shareholders' point.

jokes.text: Do you love her, or do you love the idea of her? I don't know, man. I just don't know.
jokes.setup: Serious business meeting context
jokes.punchline: Ryan's completely unrelated relationship crisis
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ryan's self-absorption and poor timing.

jokes.text: Like the time you said, 'hey look, we parked over here.' well, that was apple picking day. There's no need to yell that day. You know, I was just excited to find the car. Perfect end to a perfect day.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jim's inability to yell
jokes.punchline: The sweet domestic detail about apple picking
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Jim and Pam's relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: It's a 45-day plan. 45 days to get us back on track. 45 points. It's a 45-day, 45-point... One point per day... We get 45 points we're back in business.
jokes.setup: Michael promising a plan to save the company
jokes.punchline: The increasingly nonsensical math and logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael moment with the escalating absurdity of his made-up plan. Highly quotable and perfectly captures his character.

jokes.text: And you can take that to the bank. And limo lady, we are going completely carbon neutral.
jokes.setup: Michael's confident conclusion to his plan
jokes.punchline: The random environmental promise to the angry shareholder
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Michael's random environmental promise as damage control.

jokes.text: I love you, new york!
jokes.setup: Michael finishing his disastrous presentation
jokes.punchline: His rock star farewell despite the failure
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's delusion about how the presentation went.

jokes.text: I'm such a perfectionist that I'd kinda rather not do it at all, than do a crappy version. Simple data entry though, so there's really only one way to do it.
jokes.setup: Ryan making excuses for not working
jokes.punchline: Jim pointing out it's just basic data entry
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline with Ryan's pretentious excuse deflated by the simple reality.

jokes.text: Your new office. How great is that, right? For a job well done. Well... Not done.
jokes.setup: Jim presenting Ryan with a 'new office'
jokes.punchline: It's clearly a small enclosed space, plus Jim's correction
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag with Jim's passive-aggressive management and self-correction.

jokes.text: If I could have everyone's attention. I just figured you needed a place where you could concentrate and not be bothered by bothering people. Your new office.
jokes.setup: Jim announcing Ryan's new arrangement
jokes.punchline: Revealing the storage closet as an 'office'
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag with Jim's passive-aggressive solution. The euphemistic language makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Is there internet?
jokes.setup: Ryan locked in the small 'office'
jokes.punchline: His practical concern about connectivity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Ryan's priorities being internet access even in punishment.

jokes.text: Is there internet?
jokes.setup: Ryan being shown his closet office
jokes.punchline: Practical question about basic amenities
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan question that highlights the absurdity of the situation. Ryan's priorities are perfectly him.

jokes.text: That wasn't what you were sayin' to me. He was much more articulate and that was better. Much better.
jokes.setup: Oscar's poor performance explaining the plan
jokes.punchline: Michael's public criticism making it worse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Michael making a bad situation worse by criticizing Oscar publicly.

jokes.text: Where's the off button on this moron? Uh, I'm not a moron.
jokes.setup: Board member's frustrated insult
jokes.punchline: Michael's literal response
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline with Michael's defensive but tone-deaf response.

jokes.text: Time after time, my branch leads in sales, I have personally won over 17 dundee awards, so I am not a moron. And I'm just trying to help, you know, so you're the moron.
jokes.setup: Michael defending himself professionally
jokes.punchline: Descending to 'you're the moron'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's inability to stay professional when insulted.

jokes.text: He can take the bus. He's had his limo ride.
jokes.setup: Michael's poor performance at the meeting
jokes.punchline: The dismissive transportation downgrade
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the limo theme with Michael's comeuppance.

jokes.text: But limos are for people who make the company money not lose millions and have no plan. So we're leaving early.
jokes.setup: Being dismissed by the board
jokes.punchline: Michael's defiant but hollow comeback
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Michael trying to save face while being escorted out.

jokes.text: Did you steal that? After all, we are the only ones with anything to celebrate. All: scranton, scranton, scranton, scranton!
jokes.setup: Taking something from the meeting
jokes.punchline: Michael's justification and the group chant
jokes.explanation: Good team moment with Michael turning their dismissal into a victory.

jokes.text: Scranton, scranton, scranton, scranton! Dunder mifflin!
jokes.setup: Office workers celebrating their escape from shareholders meeting
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic chanting of their mediocre city and failing company
jokes.explanation: Good ironic celebration of their minor victory amid company collapse

jokes.text: I just want to say that I have been standing in this line, all day... And if this line is any indication of how this company is being run, then we are in big trouble!
jokes.setup: Dwight finally getting to speak at the meeting
jokes.punchline: His complaint about line management efficiency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight character moment focusing on operational efficiency instead of financial crisis.

jokes.text: A 'take-a-number' option, like they have in a deli... What about line varieties? Like an express line... For quick comments, of ten words or less - they could move much more efficiently.
jokes.setup: Dwight offering solutions to corporate crisis
jokes.punchline: All his solutions are about line management
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight character moment with his systematic approach to the wrong problem. Great escalation and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: What about ropes, along the lines, that you can hold on to?
jokes.setup: Dwight's continued line efficiency suggestions
jokes.punchline: The increasingly specific rope suggestion
jokes.explanation: Great continuation of Dwight's obsession with queue management at inappropriate time.
